calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with (trustee) Paula Bibbs.

The Court asked Ms. Navia to address the $525.00 filing fee. Ms. Navia stated that she would defer that question to Ms. Bibbs and addressed attorney fees stating that the 10 hours for the Report of Sale comprised of a 6 week period, that Ms. Navia's time was 2 hours and Mr. Merino's time was 8.6 hours, explained that they had received the initial purchase agreement, that the property was inspected and the buyer found significant issues with the foundation, that they had to renegotiate, that the backup offers fell through due to the foundation issues, that the reverse mortgage was due and they had to have discussions with the lienholder, stressed again that this was over a 6 week period, that in regards to revising the ex parte they had a difficult time trying to find the son and other family members, that regarding the continuances the first three were due to selling the real property, that there was personal property that needed to be dealt with and that in regards to the 11/24/15 & 12/29/15 continuances they will reduce the fees by $600.00 for those appearances. Ms. Bibbs reported that she has issues with her back and had been in a lot of pain, that due to this the checks sat and did not get deposited until January, that in regards to packing up the house it is a large house with 30 years of items, that the family members are not interested and she had to pack it up herself. Ms. Navia stated that if they would have hired someone to pack Ms. Bibbs would have had to be there anyway to designate family heirlooms and what could be disposed of, that when Mr. Mosley was in the hospital he had no clothes, that Ms. Bibbs had to get the clothes for the funeral, that the family members have not been willing to do much, that Ms. Bibbs had to do these tasks in order for the administration to go forward and that there have been no objections regarding her time. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Will Doyle for petitioner Paula Bibbs.

Mr. Doyle reported that Ms. Bibbs has been slowed down by a back injury and needs additional time to complete work on the accounting. He stated that she gave notice to the trust beneficiaries to come and pick up the personal property, but has to date received no response. She is trying to dispose of it now, but that needs to be done before the accounting can be completed. The Court stated that he would grant one final continuance, and the matter was continued to January 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court directed that Ms. Bibbs file an interim accounting if the full accounting could not be prepared by that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported).

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Dec-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for (trustee) Paula Bibbs.

Ms. Navia reported that they still have loose ends, that settlement proceeds which should have gone into the trust went directly to the beneficiary, that they are trying to confirm receipt with the beneficiary, that the real property removed from the home needs to be distributed or disposed of, that the beneficiaries have been given 2 weeks to pick up, that there is a vehicle that they tried to sell but will probably have to sell for scrap only and requested an additional 30 days. The Court stated that the interim accounting is 9 months overdue. The status on filing an accounting was continued to December 22, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Continued To Nov-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for (trustee) Paula Bibbs.

Ms. Navia reported that the trust will fund today, that everything was signed last week, that they intend to do a final accounting and requested a 30 day continuance. The status on filing an accounting was continued to November 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with a request from the court that it be filed by that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-02-2015 Continued To Oct-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (680 Los Palmos Drive, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs.

The Court clarified that although item 4e on the Report of Sale indicates that payment will be cash and credit that it is only cash and asked if there were anyone here to over bid on the property. Ms. Navia stated that they thought there would be an overbidder but no one is present and that she does not want to delay the sale. The Court stated that it will go forward with the notation that item 4e of the petition is incorrect. The sale was confirmed at $1,208,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on 9/3/15. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,323,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Oct-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for (trustee) Paula Bibbs.

Ms. Navia reported that the real property is encumbered by a reverse mortgage, that they received approval to sell the property earlier this month, that the property is being shown and that they are trying to sell it. The status of filing an accounting was continued to October 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-22-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-07-289745 Conservatorship Joseph Mosley.

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Resigning Trustee). Mary Jane Gordon for and with Paula Bibbs. Amy Harrington for and with Ron Mosley. Joseph Mosley in Propria Persona.

The Parties will proceed with transfer of the vehicle to Ron Mosley which is to be used as a down payment for purchase of another Family vehicle for transporting Joe Mosley. Ms. Gordon stated that it is not their intent to pursue the ancillary proceeding in Texas with regard to Mr. Mosley's .718 share/interest in the property and requested authority to execute a Quit Claim Deed. The Court directed Ms. Gordon to explore whether the other property owners would be willing to pay Mr. Mosley for his share. The status hearing was taken off calendar. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee was granted (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $245,000.00. Status on filing of bond, the inventory and the Former Trustee's final account was set for May 1, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on the Successor Trustee's first account was set for March 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Bringing Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision And Appointing Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Mosely (Trustor); Debra Dolch (Petitioner); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Ms. Dolch).

Ms. Rasch reported that Ronald Mosely (Son) had taken out two mortgages against his Father's properties to purchase five properties in Stockton, CA. Two of those properties were foreclosed and another property is currently at risk for foreclosure. Ms. Rasch also reported that there is a bond forfeiture against Mr. Mosely's residence for Ronald's failure to appear in court. The property (Mr. Mosely's residence) is titled in a Trust, but a copy of the Trust Instrument has not been located. Ms. Rasch requested the following: Authority to sell real property other than residence; waive Power of Attorney of Asset Management; waive bond for the Trust; and waive Notice to Ronald, Brett, and Raquel Mosley. The Court granted her requests for the same and granted the Petition to Bring the Trust Under Court Supervision and Appointed Debra Dolch the Successor Trustee of the Mosely Family Revocable Trust (signing the Order for same in open Court).

Further Orders:
First Accounting must be filed two weeks before: July 8, 2008.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Court Investigator: Cynthia Jones; Bailiff: F. Smith; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.
